title: Audio story
description: Machine-read source of truth for an audio story (narrated fiction / audio drama / TTS-voiced short), at projects/{slug}/audio/{lang}/{slug}.audio.json. The piece is an ordered list of cues — narration / dialogue / sfx / music — over a declared cast of voices and a declared set of sounds; the human-/producer-facing script is rendered from this into a sibling .md. The deterministic audio-story gate (scripts/check-audio-story.mjs) enforces, for EAR-ONLY consumption: every spoken cue resolves to a declared voice, every sfx/music cue resolves to a declared sound, the opening orients the listener (not cold dialogue), runtime is within tolerance of target_seconds, and a TTS-pronounceability scan — every TTS-hostile token (digit runs, currency, symbols, URLs, ALL-CAPS acronyms) must be covered by a say_as override.

keyword.description: Optional target keyword/topic (ideally backed by a *.research.json with decision: pursue).

target_seconds.description: Intended runtime in seconds. The gate checks estimated runtime (CJK-aware spoken time + sfx/music seconds) is within tolerance. Spoken time = Latin words / words_per_minute + CJK chars / chars_per_minute.

words_per_minute.description: Latin-word narration rate for the runtime estimate. Default 155 (gate). Use ~130 for slow/educational delivery.

chars_per_minute.description: CJK (中文等) narration rate for the runtime estimate — a Chinese narrator speaks ~240 字/分, not 155 "words"/分. Default 240 (gate); use ~200 for slow/educational delivery. Ignored for pure-Latin text.

voices.description: The cast. Every narration/dialogue cue must reference one of these by id; no declared voice may be left unused.

id.description: Stable voice id referenced by cues (e.g. 'narrator', 'mara').

name.description: Display name shown in the rendered cast list / speaker labels.

bio.description: Optional one-line cast description for the rendered .md's Cast list (e.g. 'the old keeper; weathered, gentle'). This is a character/role note for the reader — distinct from tts_voice, which is the concrete engine voice.

kind.description: narrator = tells the story; character = speaks dialogue. Default character.

tts_voice.description: Optional concrete TTS voice id/name to render with (e.g. an ElevenLabs/Polly voice).

sounds.description: Declared sound/music assets. Every sfx/music cue must reference one of these by id; no declared sound may be left unused.

id.description: Stable sound id referenced by sfx/music cues (e.g. 'wind', 'foghorn', 'theme').

description.description: What the sound is (shown in the rendered cue).

cues.description: Ordered cues. The FIRST cue may not be 'dialogue' (orient the listener first). narration/dialogue require voice+text; sfx/music require sound.

type.description: narration = narrator telling; dialogue = a character speaking; sfx = a sound effect; music = a music cue.

voice.description: For narration/dialogue: the voice id speaking (must be declared).

text.description: For narration/dialogue: the spoken words. Word count drives the runtime estimate and the TTS-safety scan.

sound.description: For sfx/music: the declared sound id this cue plays.

seconds.description: For sfx/music: optional duration added to the runtime estimate.

direction.description: Optional delivery note (tone/emotion) shown in the rendered script; NOT spoken, NOT counted.

say_as.description: Pronunciation overrides for TTS-hostile tokens in this cue's text. Each entry declares a substring and how to speak it (SSML interpret-as). A token flagged by the safety scan passes if it is covered here.

text.description: The exact substring in the cue text to control (e.g. '0300', 'NW', '$5').

as.description: SSML interpret-as value — how the engine should voice it.

alias.description: Optional plain spoken form (SSML sub alias), e.g. 'three a.m.' for '0300'.
